blockers: The stalled-disk monitor is exercised by pointing vttablet's --disk-write-dir at a signal-gated go-fuse loopback mount and stalling it with SIGUSR1; a container-per-component tablet has its own mount and PID namespace, so the fault-injecting FUSE mount and vttablet's write path never share a filesystem the test can gate.

Mounting FUSE inside the tablet container needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN plus /dev/fuse and fusermount3 in the image (a privileged container); the framework grants tablets neither the capability nor the device and exposes no option to add them.

Bind-propagating a host FUSE mount into the tablet container needs a shared-propagation host bind mount established before start; the framework mounts only Docker volumes (the backup volume), with no host-bind-mount option.
Linux-only (//go:build linux) and self-skips unless CI or GITHUB_ACTIONS is set, so it cannot be exercised on a Darwin developer host at all.
vitess.io/vitess/go/test/endtoend/tablet.notes: Exception: needs host-level disk fault injection the container-per-component model cannot provide. TestMain builds a go-fuse loopback helper (fuse_helper), mounts it on a host path, points vttablet --disk-write-dir at that mount, and stalls it with SIGUSR1 / clears it with SIGHUP by signaling the helper PID; the disk health monitor's blocked probe write flips the tablet to NOT_SERVING and back to SERVING. In the container model vttablet runs in its own mount and PID namespace, so reproducing this needs either a privileged tablet container (CAP_SYS_ADMIN + /dev/fuse + fusermount3) mounting FUSE in-container, or a shared-propagation host bind mount of a host FUSE mount, neither of which the framework offers. Linux-only and CI-gated (skips unless CI/GITHUB_ACTIONS). | cluster APIs: cluster.NewCluster, LocalProcessCluster.StartTopo, LocalProcessCluster.StartUnshardedKeyspace, LocalProcessCluster.Teardown, VttabletProcess.WaitForTabletStatusesForTimeout, VtctldClientProcess.ExecuteCommandWithOutput (GetFullStatus) | external deps: go-fuse loopback fuse_helper subprocess, FUSE mount (CAP_SYS_ADMIN + /dev/fuse), fusermount/fusermount3, SIGUSR1/SIGHUP signal protocol to the helper | destructive: stalls the tablet's --disk-write-dir filesystem to drive it NOT_SERVING, then clears the stall to recover | difficulty: hard

vitess.io/vitess/go/test/endtoend/transa.quarantine_reason: The migration is complete and faithful, but the reshard disruption cannot pass with this package's schema, on any framework. Reshard Create defaults to --defer-secondary-keys=true, so the target primary's copy phase runs `alter table twopc_fuzzer_insert drop key col`, which MySQL rejects with errno 1075 because `col` is auto_increment and is not the leading column of the primary key. Only errno 1091 is tolerated, so both streams stay at an empty position and the catchup wait times out. The same ALTER is rejected by stock mysql:8.0.42 and mysql:8.4, so this is not a container artifact. Upstream tracks it as vitessio/vitess#18965.
